title: Broadcom Symantec ProxySG SSL Inspection Bypass Category Audit
dataSources: `sourcetype=bluecoat:proxysg:access:syslog` ProxySG access logs including URL category and SSL inspection outcome fields
app: Blue Coat ProxySG Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase) or Splunk Connect for Syslog with TA field extractions
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="bluecoat:proxysg:access:syslog" earliest=-24h
| eval cat=coalesce(cs_categories, category, url_category, "")
| eval ssl_state=coalesce(ssl_inspection, ssl_scan_result, inspection_status, "")
| eval user=coalesce(cs_username, user, suser, "")
| where match(lower(cat), "(?i)financial|health|ssl|tunnel|bypass|no.inspect") OR match(lower(ssl_state), "(?i)bypass|tunnel|failed|not.inspected")
| stats count sum(sc_bytes) as bytes by user cat ssl_state
| eval mb=round(bytes/1048576,2)
| sort -count
description: SSL inspection bypass rules reduce visibility into encrypted threats and data exfiltration. Auditing ProxySG traffic that skips inspection or uses high-risk categories validates that exceptions are narrow and justified.
value: Supports zero-trust web policies and regulatory expectations for encrypted traffic visibility without blocking every false-positive banking site.
implementation: (1) Confirm `cs_categories` and SSL inspection fields extract per your SGOS and TA version. (2) Maintain an approved-bypass lookup for known break-glass URLs. (3) Weekly SecOps review of top users. (4) Correlate with DLP and CASB signals. (5) Tune categories for local privacy law.
visualization: Table (user, category, inspection state, MB transferred), bar chart (top categories), timeline.
grandmaExplanation: We review Broadcom ProxySG web logs where security scanning was skipped so hidden malware or leaks in encrypted sites are not missed.
